extraction.one_line_summary: MADRAZO Angel won from breakaway after being dropped multiple times on final climb, yoyo'ing back then attacking inside final kilometer.
tactical_patterns: Madrazo repeatedly dropped on 10%+ finish yet recovered and attacked sub-1km, revealing breakaway stamina matters more than sustained climbing power.
Sepp Kuss domestique work neutralized Valverde's acceleration for Roglic, signaling Jumbo's willingness to control without needing Roglic to respond directly.
Lopez attacked 3km from summit isolating himself from all GC rivals, gaining only 12 seconds on Valverde/Roglic suggesting group dynamics trump solo efforts.
Hugh Carthy pacing for Uran dropped teammate Higuita, showing EF prioritized Uran over multiple cards despite having three climbers in form.

rider_signals.MADRAZO Angel: positive: Won despite being dropped 4+ times on final climb, showing exceptional recovery ability and tactical patience to attack under 1km
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: Isolated all GC rivals with 3km attack but gained only 12 seconds, showing explosive power yet limited time-gaining capacity solo
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Neutralized Valverde's attack without Roglic responding, revealing high-altitude climbing strength beyond pure domestique role
rider_signals.ROCHE Nicolas: negative: Dropped from race lead before summit finish proper began, confirming GC hold was unsustainable against climber acceleration
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Youngest rider limiting losses to seconds behind Valverde/Roglic group, signaling unexpected GC climbing credentials
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: negative: Dropped by own teammate Carthy's pacing for Uran, revealing hierarchy within EF despite appearing to have multiple options
